regression_risks: run_building_once still emits only the terminal building event after write_single_building_evidence at support/operator/run.py:1158-1200; _emit_brick_grain_step_events is not called on that public surface. If run_building_once is in scope, brick-grain replies are missing there.

The receipt/return/gate times are collapsed: run.py and walker_kernel.py emit brick_received, brick_returned, and gate_passed together after step-output write, and both helpers set received_at and returned_at from the same recorded_at value at run.py:1992-1998 and walker_kernel.py:1157-1164. This does not prove emission at the distinct receipt, return, and Link transition write seams.

Dynamic walker numbering uses len(step_results)+1 at support/operator/walker_kernel.py:1759-1761, which is execution order, not declared sequence position. Reroute, retry, or fan-in re-entry can render the wrong circled prefix.

Fan-in wording is not implemented in the thread reply gate note. The gate-note helpers at run.py:2003-2008 and walker_kernel.py:1168-1173 only render 홀드 or 통과→다음스텝, while fan-in wait can hold before a step reply at walker_kernel.py:1473-1498. 합류 대기/성립 is not covered.

The checker’s disposition_applied probe manually renders a disposition packet at support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py:2830-2853 rather than exercising a real hold-resume walker seam, so the declared hold-resume delivery path is not proven.

The checker’s brick-grain happy path is a single run_building_plan probe at support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py:2716-2734; it does not cover run_building_once, dynamic declared-position numbering, fan-in wording, or adapter-error retry append behavior.
